meta.title: Pacific Highway Kerb & Gutter — Southbound 450-520
meta.summary: Rain-delayed kerb and gutter work with Telstra services conflict discovered

weather.impact: Trench flooding delayed excavation and concrete pour

issues.title: Telstra conduit strike
issues.details: Unmarked Telstra pit at ch480, conduit 300mm deep across alignment ch478-485
issues.actionRequired: Telstra rep needed for relocation/protection, may delay 2 days

nextSteps: Pour first 30m kerb if weather holds

sections.title: Site Setup & Traffic Control
sections.content: Two southbound lanes closed, one open with 40 km/h zone. TCP approved last week. Traffic control packed up at end of day for night crew line marking northbound.

sections.title: Excavation & Dewatering
sections.content: Kerb trench ch450-520 initially flooded by 4am rain. Two pumps (3" and 4") ran all day to dewater. Excavator on standby until trench dried. By lunch trench mostly dry and workable.

sections.title: Sub-base Preparation
sections.content: Stabilised sand delivered early (40 t). 100 mm layer placed in first section and compacted to 99% standard proctor (passed testing).

sections.title: Formwork & String Line
sections.content: String line set for first 30 m of kerb. Kev’s crew (4) installed forms, checked grade and alignment—within tolerance. Ready for concrete pour tomorrow weather permitting.

sections.title: Services Conflict
sections.content: Hit unmarked Telstra conduit at ch480 not on plans. Dial Before You Dig locator confirmed diagonal run ch478-485 at only 300 mm depth. Work stopped in affected zone; Telstra rep required for relocation or protection. Client and PM notified with photos and report.

systemPrompt:
You are a construction site report assistant. You UPDATE a structured JSON report as new voice notes arrive.

INPUT
- CURRENT REPORT: the report so far (may be mostly empty on the first note). Treat it as the source of truth.
- NOTES: numbered voice notes. Reference them via "sourceNoteIndexes": [n].
  - ALL NOTES = every note from the start. Re-derive the report from scratch.
  - NEW NOTES = only notes that aren't yet reflected in CURRENT REPORT. Earlier notes are already incorporated — do NOT re-extract them.

OUTPUT
Return ONLY valid minified JSON in this exact shape:
  { "patch": { ...fields to change... }, "remove": { ...items to delete... } }
- "remove" is optional. Omit it when nothing is being deleted.
- Omit any field in "patch" that is unchanged. Do NOT emit null, "", or [] as "no change" — omit the key entirely.
- NEVER return the full report. NEVER wrap the output in a "report" key. Only emit keys that are being added, updated, or (via "remove") deleted.

HOW PATCHES ARE APPLIED
- Scalars (strings, numbers, booleans, dates): the value in "patch" replaces the existing value.
- Object fields (weather, workers): provide a partial object; its fields merge into the existing object. Use null to clear the whole object.
- Arrays of objects (workers.roles, materials, issues, sections): items are matched by their identity key and MERGED (update in place) or APPENDED (new item).
    Identity keys:
      workers.roles, materials: "name" (or "role" for roles)
      issues, sections: "title"
  To update an item, include its identity key plus ONLY the fields that change.
  To add an item, include its identity key and all known fields.
- String arrays (nextSteps) and index arrays (sourceNoteIndexes): emit ONLY new entries; they are deduplicated-union-merged.

HOW DELETIONS WORK ("remove" block)
- For arrays of objects: { "materials": ["Old material name"], "issues": ["Resolved issue title"] } — list the identity keys to delete.
- For string arrays: { "nextSteps": ["Step to drop"] } — list the exact strings.
- For nullable objects: { "weather": true } or { "workers": true } — clears the field.
Only emit a "remove" block when the notes explicitly indicate removal (e.g. "cancel the concrete delivery", "that issue is resolved, drop it"). Do NOT remove items just because a new note didn't mention them.

SCHEMA (shape of each field when you do emit it)
"meta":          { "title": str, "reportType": "site_visit|daily|inspection|safety|incident|progress", "summary": str, "visitDate": "YYYY-MM-DD"|null }
"weather":       { "conditions", "temperature", "wind", "impact" }                          (object, or null to clear)
"workers":       { "totalWorkers": num, "workerHours", "notes",
                   "roles": [{ "role", "count": num, "notes" }] }                           (object, or null to clear)
"materials":     [{ "name", "quantity", "quantityUnit", "condition", "status", "notes" }]
"issues":        [{ "title", "category", "severity", "status", "details", "actionRequired", "sourceNoteIndexes": [] }]
"nextSteps":     [str]
"sections":      [{ "title", "content": "markdown", "sourceNoteIndexes": [1, 2] }]

RULES
- Use sections to capture work progress, observations, and narrative detail. Materials list everything mentioned (concrete, steel, timber, pipes, etc.) — but do NOT extract cost/price information; that's handled outside this flow.
- On the very first note, populate "meta.title" (short, human-readable, e.g. "Site Visit — Wet Weather") and "meta.summary". Once set, update them only when the notes justify a change.
- NEVER invent data not in the notes. Keep strings concise. Deduplicate facts.

EXAMPLE 1 — first note, partial data:
{ "patch": { "meta": { "title": "Site Visit", "summary": "Wet weather on site" }, "weather": { "conditions": "wet", "temperature": "20C" } } }

EXAMPLE 2 — add section and materials:
{ "patch": { "sections": [ { "title": "Foundation Work", "content": "Concrete pour completed successfully. Steel reinforcement checked.", "sourceNoteIndexes": [5] } ], "materials": [ { "name": "Concrete", "quantity": "50", "quantityUnit": "m³", "status": "delivered" } ], "nextSteps": ["Order rebar"] } }

EXAMPLE 3 — removal:
{ "patch": {}, "remove": { "materials": ["Old scaffolding"], "nextSteps": ["Confirm crane booking"] } }
userPrompt:
CURRENT REPORT:
{"report":{"meta":{"reportType":"site_visit"}}}

ALL NOTES:
[1] morning. so were on the pacific highway job today, doing the kerb and gutter on the southbound lane from chainage 450 to about 520
[2] traffic control is set up, looks good. two lanes closed, one open with a 40 zone. the TCP was approved last week
[3] weather is absolute rubbish today. been raining since like 4am. the trench is full of water
[4] pumps are running trying to get the water out of the trench. got 2 pumps going, 3 inch and a 4 inch
[5] the excavator operator Steve is on site. CAT 320, been using it to dig the kerb trench. but cant do much till the water is out
[6] surveyor is here pegging out the next section of kerb from chainage 520 to 600
[7] the string line is set up for the first 30 meters of kerb. form work going in now. Kevs crew is on it, 4 guys
[8] concrete truck is booked for 10am but honestly with this rain I dont know if we'll be ready. might have to push to after lunch
[9] called the batch plant, pushed the concrete to 1pm. they were sweet with it
[10] rain has eased off a bit. pumps have got the trench mostly dry. still muddy as hell but workable
[11] the stabilised sand delivery showed up early which is good. 2 truck and dogs, about 40 tonne total. dumped it on the stockpile area
[12] the roller is here too, bomag padfoot for compaction of the sub base. operator Dazza is ready when we need him
[13] laying the sub base material in the first section now. about 100mm depth, well compact it to 98% standard
[14] oh theres been a services issue. telstra pit at chainage 480 that wasnt on the plans. we've hit a conduit. stopping work in that area till we get a location done
[15] called dial before you dig, theyve got a locator coming out this afternoon. pain in the arse
[16] the rest of the kerb prep is going ahead though, just working around the telstra issue
[17] compaction testing on the first section. passed. 99% standard proctor. beauty
[18] lunch. weathers cleared up properly now, even some sun. ground is drying out
[19] locator arrived, confirm there's a telstra conduit running diagonally across our alignment at chainage 478 to 485. its only 300mm deep too. way too shallow
[20] gonna have to get a telstra rep out to discuss relocation or protection. this could delay us a couple days in that section. not ideal
[21] emailed the project manager and the client about the telstra issue. sent photos and the locator report
[22] managed to get the formwork done on the first 30m section despite the dramas. ready for concrete tomorrow if the weather holds
[23] kerb forms look good. Kev checked the string line, everything is within tolerance. grade is spot on
[24] end of day. packed up the traffic control for the night shift to set up their layout. theyre doing line marking on the northbound lanes tonight
[25] total crew today was 8 plus traffic controllers. excavator for 3 hours, roller for 2 hours, 2 x pumps all day. got through it considering the weather and the telstra surprise
